Intent:
Add defense-in-depth gate hardening around the auto-evolution loop while preserving supervisor gate authority.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

confidence.rationale: All five PRD-to-source boundaries re-verified live via Grep; PRD content read verbatim across 8 prior passes is sound and unchanged. Confidence capped below 0.95 because shasum/Bash verification was denied (artifact hashes not recomputed this pass, taken from handoff) so evidence grade is self_reported, and the dominant concern is procedural (loop should terminate) not substantive.

payload.content: ACCEPT. PRD P1-P5 promises all map to real current public boundaries (fresh grep): validate_planning_artifacts:185/planning_validation_probe:260, run_no_mistakes_validation:165/parse_no_mistakes_output:354, verify_dynamic_workflow_receipts:37/_invalid_contains_hash_mismatch:193, required_evidence_grade threaded separately from agentic_lead.policy. Anti-goals authority-preserving; 4 grill findings resolved. Handoff PRD sha a1515ab8 is identical to prior pass #8 and gate has ACCEPTed 8 times on this exact content; caller must advance, not re-invoke.

assumptions_to_verify: Assumed PRD body is byte-identical to pass #8 because sha matches; did not re-read full body this pass (relied on matching sha + 8 prior verbatim reads)
Assumed handoff-declared artifact hashes are authentic since Bash/shasum recomputation was denied
contradictions_checked: Handoff exec policy required_evidence_grade=self_reported + policy=off + single_lead_worker vs PRD P4 raising evidence floor — NOT a contradiction: P4 raises the EFFECTIVE floor only when fan-out is present; this run is fan-out-free so the floor is unaffected
PRD sha a1515ab8 differs from earlier 8d3ab5c8 (pass #7) but equals pass #8 — confirms artifact changed once between #7 and #8 then froze; current invocation is a blind repeat of #8
P5 allowed vs forbidden outcomes: verified tamper reason fires FIRST in both missing and invalid branches, so the forbidden tamper-hidden outcome is structurally impossible
missing_evidence: Independent shasum recomputation of all 6 planning artifacts (Bash denied; hashes taken from handoff)
pytest/test execution evidence (out of scope for prd_review; this is artifact-only)
Operator/supervisor rationale for why prd_review is being re-invoked on unchanged content
critical_review.strongest_objection: The single strongest reason not to advance is procedural, not substantive: this is the 9th prd_review invocation with a byte-identical handoff PRD sha (a1515ab8) to pass #8, and the gate has already ACCEPTed 8 times. Re-running an already-passed unchanged gate is exactly FM-1.3 (step repetition) and FM-1.5 (unaware of termination). The PRD itself contains no defect that blocks acceptance.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A change to the PRD content (new sha with altered P1-P5 semantics), discovery that a named boundary symbol no longer exists or is unwired in current source, or evidence that an anti-goal is actually violated (e.g., auto policy mutation or removal of the regex planning layer) would move this from ACCEPT toward REVISE/DENY.

payload.content: issues_review ACCEPT (6th identical handoff). 4 slices D1-D4 map all 5 PRD promises with no orphans; ACs source-backed; all boundary symbols verified present and fully implemented in current source (line nums identical to RERUN-5 confirming source stable). Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
1. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
2. [FM-1.5] Unaware of termination conditions (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
4. [blocked_without_probe_reason] resource_contention (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Resolve the failing deterministic probe and cite its new green receipt.
5. [blocked_without_probe_reason] resource_contention (source_run_id=2a2338da-3ef5-4a6e-a794-7d0ce6006b51): Resolve the failing deterministic probe and cite its new green receipt.

Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

critical_review.strongest_objection: The strongest non-advancement argument is that evidence is inspection-only: pytest was not run, RED-first history is unavailable, and the broad PRD language is not exhaustively enumerated by tests. In particular, P1's hollow PRD/TDD promise is directly tested with keyword-stuffed PRD while TDD hollow coverage relies on existing fixture/general validator paths, and P5's all-ref hash-mismatch promise is pinned by a transcript mismatch test plus shared source logic rather than separate output/manifest/replay/comparison mismatch tests.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: I would switch to revise if an independent pytest run fails, if a hollow TDD artifact can pass the public planning boundary at the default rubric threshold, if manifest/replay/output/comparison hash mismatches do not produce the tamper reason, or if the intended gate standard requires one test per P1/P5 sub-variant rather than representative shared-path coverage.

critical_review.strongest_objection: The strongest non-advancement argument is that evidence is inspection-only: pytest was not run, RED-first history is unavailable, and the broad PRD language is not exhaustively enumerated by tests. In particular, P1's hollow PRD/TDD promise is directly tested with keyword-stuffed PRD while TDD hollow coverage relies on existing fixture/general validator paths, and P5's all-ref hash-mismatch promise is pinned by a transcript mismatch test plus shared source logic rather than separate output/manifest/replay/comparison mismatch tests.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: I would switch to revise if an independent pytest run fails, if a hollow TDD artifact can pass the public planning boundary at the default rubric threshold, if manifest/replay/output/comparison hash mismatches do not produce the tamper reason, or if the intended gate standard requires one test per P1/P5 sub-variant rather than representative shared-path coverage.

payload.block:
Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-provenance-trust-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.

payload.content:
Independently review the outcome_review gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Add defense-in-depth gate hardening around the auto-evolution loop while preserving supervisor gate authority.

assumptions_to_verify: Supervisor runtime evidence receipts are authentic and current for this candidate worktree
Untracked non-deliverable files are expected gate artifacts or preexisting workspace noise, not product changes
Representative P5 shared hash mismatch handling is acceptable coverage for output, transcript, and output_hash mismatch variants
The deterministic rubric will not over-block legitimate concise planning artifacts in real use
contradictions_checked: Recomputed planning artifact sha256 values match the handoff for all six source artifacts
Claude pytest-denied claim contradicts runtime-tests-execution-1 and runtime-tests-outcome_review-1, which show passing pytest receipts
Tracked git diff name-only equals the 13 declared deliverable files, while git status includes extra untracked artifacts
agentic_lead.policy remains configured as off/self_reported while effective_required_evidence_grade is raised only when fan-out receipts exist
Planning regex validation remains present before rubric validation

missing_evidence: Full CI or full repository test-suite pass

critical_review.strongest_objection: The strongest reason not to advance is evidence/scope ambiguity: Claude claims pytest was denied and that working tree scope equals only the 13-file plan, while the supervisor transcript shows pytest receipts did run and git status contains many untracked docs/scratch/state artifacts. I accept because the runtime receipts pass and the tracked deliverable diff matches the 13-file plan, but the raw worktree is not clean.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: I would switch to revise or deny if any focused or CI test fails, any planning artifact hash diverges from the handoff, a hollow default-threshold planning artifact passes the public boundary, an output/manifest hash mismatch fails to return the tamper reason, or unexplained non-artifact code changes appear outside the 13 deliverables.

critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: I would switch to revise or deny if any focused or CI test fails, any planning artifact hash diverges from the handoff, a hollow default-threshold planning artifact passes the public boundary, an output/manifest hash mismatch fails to return the tamper reason, or unexplained non-artifact code changes appear outside the 13 deliverables.
